FY 2025/26 Projects

FY 2025-26: Active Projects
Click to add photo
In Progress

GDS Network Refresh

High School & LMS
Technology

A comprehensive overhaul of GDS's network infrastructure across both buildings, replacing outdated Cat5 cabling with Cat6, upgrading all switches, routers, and wireless access points, and installing new firewalls in failover configuration between campuses. Users will automatically connect to a unified network across campus, which will no longer require user login or switching between each buildings WiFi.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Lightning Protection System

High School
Facilities

Completion of the campus-wide lightning protection system that began earlier this year. This second phase, already under contract with Regional Electric, extends coverage to the remaining campus structures.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Wheelchair Lift Replacement

High School
Facilities

Replacement of the high school's aging wheelchair lift to ensure reliable, code-compliant accessibility for students and visitors with mobility needs.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Gutters, Soffits & Landscaping

High School
Facilities

Repair and replacement of gutters and soffits on the high school building, combined with plant beautification to improve the building's exterior and protect against water damage.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Soccer Field Drain Repair

High School
Grounds

Repair of the drainage system serving the high school soccer field to reduce standing water after rain events and restore full use of the playing surface.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Field Storage Shed

LMS
Athletics

A dedicated storage shed on the LMS field for Lower School, Middle School, and PE equipment, keeping recess and athletics gear organized, secure, and accessible for daily use.

In Progress

Business Office Chairs

High School
Operations

Replacement of seven worn ergonomic desk chairs in the Business Office to support staff comfort during the workday.
Click to add photo
In Progress

Kiln & Electrical

High School
Arts

Replacement of one of the high school ceramics kilns, both approximately 30 years old and heavily rusted, along with the electrical upgrades needed to support the new unit.

Technology

Full audio-visual buildout for the new 4th floor classroom, including projection, display, and sound systems for teaching and large-group presentations.


Electrical infrastructure work for the new 4th floor A/V installation, including dedicated circuits and conduit to power projection and sound systems.

Upcoming

Field Accessible and Ground Floor Bathrooms

High School
Facilities

Creation of new  High School ground floor restrooms, creating single-user accessible bathrooms with direct outdoor access and interior access options. The redesigned layout introduces a reception area with card reader access controls for a dedicated Faculty restroom and a new door with privacy film sidelights for improved security and user comfort.
Click to add photo
Upcoming

Security Vestibule

High School
Facilities

Construction of a new security vestibule at the High School main entrance, replacing the existing atrium storefront with a new double-door storefront system. The redesigned entry will include a dedicated security desk, an improved visitor management flow to enhance campus safety. The adjacent area will be converted to a lounge for community use.
Click to add photo
Upcoming

New Deans Suite & Offices

High School
Facilities

Conversion of a first-floor High School classroom into a new suite comprising three private offices and a focus room, totaling approximately 655 square feet. New doors provide direct access from the student lounge, creating a more functional and accessible home for the deans team.
Click to add photo
Upcoming

Unified Nurses Suite

Lower/Middle School
Facilities

Renovation and expansion of the Lower/Middle School nurse's suite to include two single private offices, a dedicated waiting area, three beds, a new base cabinet with sink, and glass sidelights at both new entry doors.

Adding hinged lids with slow-close hardware to the wooden benches in the B1 hallway so students can store sports equipment inside, similar to the existing lost-and-found bench design.
